title: picris hieracioides var. umbellata
intro: Picris hieracioides var. umbellata, also known as the Lesser Hawkbit, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the dandelion family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its sesquiterpene lactone compounds. The health benefits of Picris hieracioides var. umbellata include its ability to treat digestive issues, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, while also exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate various health concerns.
